notable_pick_trades: Crosby trade rescinded — Ravens retain 2026 and 2027 first-round picks. Four compensatory picks awarded (2 fifth-rounders, 2 seventh-rounders).
roster_context: New head coach Jesse Minter takes over an 8-9 team after John Harbaugh was fired. The Ravens lost a staggering number of starters in free agency — C Tyler Linderbaum, TE Isaiah Likely, TE Charlie Kolar, P Jordan Stout, S Alohi Gilman, S Ar'Darius Washington, EDGE Dre'Mont Jones, EDGE David Ojabo, EDGE Kyle Van Noy, and RB Keaton Mitchell. DT Nnamdi Madubuike's 2026 status remains uncertain after a potentially career-threatening neck injury. Baltimore signed EDGE Trey Hendrickson, IOL John Simpson, S Jaylinn Hawkins, CB Chidobe Awuzie (re-signed), QB Tyler Huntley (re-signed), and TE Durham Smythe. The interior OL is in shambles after Linderbaum's departure and guard play ranked 32nd in 2025. WR room is thin behind Zay Flowers with Rashod Bateman in trade rumors after a career-low 224-yard 2025 season. Eric DeCosta has signaled the draft is the primary roster-building tool this offseason.

needs.explanation: Tyler Linderbaum signed a record $81M deal with the Raiders, leaving a massive void at center. The starting guards ranked dead last (32nd) in pass block win rate in 2025. John Simpson was signed to address one guard spot, but the Ravens still need a starting center and potentially another guard. Emery Jones Jr. (2025 3rd-round pick) and Andrew Vorhees are the only remaining interior options.
needs.current_starter: John Simpson (G, signed FA), Emery Jones Jr. or Andrew Vorhees (G), VACANT at center (Corey Bullock re-signed as depth)
needs.depth: Extremely thin. No proven center on roster after Linderbaum's departure. Simpson is the only proven starter among remaining guards.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — center or versatile interior lineman

needs.explanation: DeAndre Hopkins departed via voided contract. Rashod Bateman had a career-low 224 yards in 2025 and is the subject of active trade rumors. Zay Flowers (back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons, 2x Pro Bowler) is the only reliable weapon. Behind him, the depth chart features Devontez Walker, LaJohntay Wester, and Cornelius Johnson — none proven NFL contributors. The new offense under OC Declan Doyle will be more up-tempo, demanding better receiving talent.
needs.current_starter: Zay Flowers (WR1), Rashod Bateman (WR2, may be traded)
needs.depth: Devontez Walker, LaJohntay Wester, Cornelius Johnson — very thin after Flowers
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — top WR prospect to pair with Flowers

needs.explanation: Marlon Humphrey is entering the final year of his deal and will turn 33 in 2026. Nate Wiggins (2024 1st-rounder) showed promise but is still developing. Chidobe Awuzie was re-signed on a one-year deal as a stopgap CB3. The secondary was inconsistent in 2025 and DeCosta has said CB is a 'big need every year.' Baltimore needs to invest in the long-term future of the position.
needs.current_starter: Marlon Humphrey (CB1, final year of deal), Nate Wiggins (CB2), Chidobe Awuzie (CB3, one-year deal)
needs.depth: Adequate for 2026 with Awuzie re-signing, but no long-term answer behind Humphrey

needs.explanation: Nnamdi Madubuike missed 15 games with a career-threatening neck injury and his 2026 availability remains deeply uncertain despite positive signals. The defense collapsed without him — the Ravens finished with just 30 sacks (30th in NFL). Travis Jones signed an extension and John Jenkins was re-signed, but the Ravens need a disruptive 3-tech pass rusher in case Madubuike cannot return or is limited. Broderick Washington was also limited to 3 games in 2025.
needs.current_starter: Nnamdi Madubuike (status TBD — $31M cap hit, neck injury), Travis Jones (NT), John Jenkins (rotational)
needs.depth: Aeneas Peebles, David Olajiga — extremely thin behind starters. If Madubuike can't play, this becomes a Tier 1 need.

needs.explanation: Signing Trey Hendrickson (4-year, $112M) was a massive upgrade and addresses the primary pass rush need. However, the Ravens lost Dre'Mont Jones (Patriots), David Ojabo (Dolphins), and Kyle Van Noy (UFA, unsigned). Behind Hendrickson, the depth is very young: 2nd-year Mike Green, Tavius Robinson, and Adisa Isaac (4 games in 2 seasons). The Ravens need a complementary edge rusher — Hendrickson alone cannot carry the pass rush, especially given his weakness in run defense.
needs.depth: Tavius Robinson, Adisa Isaac, Kaimon Rucker — Green is promising but the cupboard is thin

needs.explanation: Isaiah Likely signed with the Giants ($40M) and Charlie Kolar signed with the Chargers ($24.3M). Mark Andrews remains the TE1 but is a 3x Pro Bowler entering his age-31 season. Durham Smythe was signed as a blocking TE from Chicago. The Ravens need a receiving TE2 to complement Andrews — this has been a two-TE-heavy offense historically.
needs.depth: No viable receiving TE behind Andrews after Likely and Kolar departed

needs.explanation: Ronnie Stanley (LT, 32 years old) is signed through 2027 on a 3-year, $60M deal and played 16 games in 2025. Roger Rosengarten (RT, 2024 draft pick) is the starting right tackle. However, Stanley's age and injury history (missed significant time 2020-2022) mean the Ravens should consider future planning here. Bleacher Report named Stanley the team's 'Top Trade Asset' this offseason.
needs.depth: Carson Vinson (developmental), Jared Penning — serviceable but no swing tackle depth

needs.explanation: Roquan Smith is elite and signed long-term. Trenton Simpson enters his 3rd year as the other starter. Teddye Buchanan provides depth. Jake Hummel departed (Raiders). The position is solid but could use a special teams contributor and developmental depth piece.
needs.depth: Teddye Buchanan — adequate but light after Hummel's departure

free_agency_impact: Lost C Tyler Linderbaum (Raiders, 3yr/$81M) — CRITICAL. Creates massive IOL need at center
Lost TE Isaiah Likely (Giants, 3yr/$40M) — creates TE2 need behind Mark Andrews
Lost TE Charlie Kolar (Chargers, 3yr/$24.3M) — deepens TE need, especially in run blocking
Lost EDGE Dre'Mont Jones (Patriots, 3yr/$39M) — thins edge depth
Lost EDGE David Ojabo (Dolphins, 1yr deal) — further thins edge group
Lost EDGE Kyle Van Noy (UFA, unsigned) — veteran pass rusher departed after 2-sack 2025
Lost S Alohi Gilman (Chiefs, 3yr/$24.75M) — major loss of 3rd safety starter
Lost S Ar'Darius Washington (Giants, 1yr deal) — backup safety gone
Lost P Jordan Stout (Giants, 3yr/$12.3M record deal) — All-Pro punter departed
Lost RB Keaton Mitchell (not tendered, signed with Chargers) — backup RB depth thinned
Signed EDGE Trey Hendrickson (4yr/$112M) — MASSIVE. Addresses #1 need at pass rusher, had 17.5 sacks in back-to-back years
Signed IOL John Simpson (3yr/$30M) — addresses one guard spot, started 17 games at LG in 2023
Signed S Jaylinn Hawkins (2yr/$10M) — replaces Gilman as 3rd safety alongside Hamilton and Starks
Re-signed CB Chidobe Awuzie (1yr deal) — retains CB3 who had lowest completion% allowed among Ravens CBs
Re-signed QB Tyler Huntley (2yr/$5M) — retains reliable backup who won 2 starts in 2025
Signed TE Durham Smythe (blocking TE from Bears) — replaces Kolar's blocking role
Crosby trade rescinded — Ravens get back 2026 and 2027 first-round picks due to failed physical
scheme_fit_notes: Under new HC Jesse Minter (former Chargers DC), Baltimore will run an aggressive, multiple-front defense that prioritizes disruption up front. Minter's scheme asks edge rushers to be versatile — not just pin-their-ears-back speed rushers — which makes sturdy, high-motor EDGE prospects who can set the edge against the run valuable. Interior DL who can generate pressure as 3-techs are paramount, especially with Madubuike's uncertain status. On offense, new OC Declan Doyle (from Bears) will push a faster tempo — the Bears ran the most plays per game in 2025, while the Ravens ranked 29th. This elevates the need for WRs who can run the full route tree and operate both inside and outside. The run-heavy identity (Derrick Henry, Lamar Jackson) means IOL prospects must excel in zone-blocking schemes. Baltimore traditionally plays three safeties, making the 3rd safety role important — Hawkins fills this for now.
